questions.questionText: The most commonly injured ligament of the knee is:
questions.optionB: Anterior cruciate ligament (ACL — most commonly injured knee ligament in sports, non-contact pivoting mechanism, positive Lachman/anterior drawer/pivot shift tests)
questions.explanation: Core concept: ACL = most commonly injured knee ligament, especially in sports. Why correct: ACL injury: non-contact pivoting/deceleration (football, basketball), contact valgus force. Heard/felt 'pop,' immediate swelling (hemarthrosis), instability. Clinical tests: Lachman (best single test — most sensitive), anterior drawer, pivot shift (most specific for ACL tear). Why A wrong: PCL — less common, 'dashboard injury' (posterior tibial force). Why C wrong: LCL — varus stress injury, less common. Why D wrong: Not a commonly tested structure. Clinical relevance: MRI: best diagnostic modality. Associated injuries: 'unhappy/O'Donoghue's triad' = ACL + MCL + medial meniscus (though lateral meniscus more commonly associated with acute ACL in modern literature). Treatment: reconstruction (hamstring/patellar tendon autograft) for young active patients. Exam trap: Lachman = MOST SENSITIVE; Pivot shift = MOST SPECIFIC for ACL. Anterior drawer = most commonly performed but less sensitive. Memory aid: 'ACL = Athletes' Commonly Lost ligament (pivoting sports).' Reference: Maheshwari – Sports Medicine.
questions.conceptTag: ACL most common knee ligament Lachman pivot shift
